rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban in any curriculum or training surface.
No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.

rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 35, 51, 56, 73, 79: Boris-direct reframes; line 27: Martin strategic framing; line 19: role-shift narrative — AE101 voice quintet balanced throughout

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 31–45: Uncle Bob quotes verbatim with X link citations; no uncredited paraphrase detected

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 13–19: third-person narrative about Uncle Bob's learning journey, not about student; no teacher framing or "the student" detected

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Terms used ('agentic failure mode', 'acceptance tests', 'unit tests', 'TDD', 'mutation testing', 'CRAP scores', 'modularity', 'architecture') are all earned in body on first use with concrete explanation

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No defensive framing, no 'not an aspirational X' or 'unlike vendor' positioning detected; teaching register throughout

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.evidence: AE101 context: 'agentic' lands at line 27 after concrete example; 'Clean Code' is anchored to Uncle Bob's framing; all technical terms earned in situ without OOB vocabulary

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 47–65: each testing/metrics discipline introduced with concrete explanation before the name ('Tests that define externally visible behavior', 'Unit tests make small behaviors hard to casually break', etc.)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'the student' references; line 19 'The programmer' is descriptive role-language, not second-person narrative imperative
This is supplementary reading (referenced from Module 5), not a module Big Idea or Key Concepts section; mood contract lives in the module file, not the reference doc
